
3. Fix dashboard.py comment referencing AuditEntry type
4. Delete orphaned test files:
   - backend/tests/test_alerts.py
   - backend/tests/test_search.py
   - e2e tests: alerts.spec.ts, search.spec.ts
5. Update backend/tests/conftest.py to remove deleted feature fixtures
6. Clean backend/load-tests/ files removing deleted endpoint references
tasks.testStrategy: Run grep searches for deleted feature names across codebase to ensure complete removal. Verify all imports resolve correctly. Run linter to catch any broken references.

subtasks.description: Remove references to deleted features from backend Python files including feedback.py and accuracy_tracking.py

Also fix dashboard.py comment referencing AuditEntry type.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run Python linter to ensure no import errors. Execute unit tests for feedback and accuracy_tracking modules to verify they still function correctly without deleted features.

subtasks.testStrategy: Run frontend build to catch any TypeScript errors. Manually verify the learning page loads without errors and deleted features are not visible in the UI.

subtasks.title: Delete orphaned test files
subtasks.description: Remove test files for deleted features including backend unit tests and e2e tests
subtasks.details: Delete backend/tests/test_alerts.py, backend/tests/test_search.py, and e2e test files alerts.spec.ts and search.spec.ts. These files test features that no longer exist in the codebase.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run full test suite after deletion to ensure no other tests depend on these files. Verify test runner doesn't report missing test files.

subtasks.title: Clean up test fixtures and configuration
subtasks.description: Update test configuration files to remove fixtures and references to deleted features
subtasks.details: Update backend/tests/conftest.py to remove any pytest fixtures related to deleted features. This includes removing fixture functions for alerts, search, impact analysis, compliance checklist, self assessment, AML case manager, suitability assessment, and regulatory intelligence.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run pytest with verbose output to ensure all fixtures load correctly. Check that no tests fail due to missing fixtures.

subtasks.title: Clean load test files
subtasks.description: Remove references to deleted endpoints from load testing scripts
subtasks.details: Update all files in backend/load-tests/ directory to remove any references to deleted feature endpoints. This includes removing test scenarios for regulation search, alerts, impact analysis, compliance checklist, self assessment, AML case manager, suitability assessment, and regulatory intelligence endpoints.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run grep searches for each deleted feature name across the entire codebase to ensure complete removal. Execute a sample load test to verify scripts run without errors.

tasks.title: Audit and Verify Database Schema
tasks.description: Validate all required tables exist in live Supabase instance and document current state
tasks.details:
1. Connect to live Supabase instance and run schema inspection:
```sql
SELECT table_name FROM information_schema.tables 
WHERE table_schema = 'public' ORDER BY table_name;
```
2. Compare against expected 28 tables from migrations
3. For each table, check row counts:
```sql
SELECT 'table_name' as table, COUNT(*) as row_count FROM table_name;
```
4. Verify RLS policies exist:
```sql
SELECT tablename, policyname FROM pg_policies WHERE schemaname = 'public';
```
5. Document findings in a database_audit.md file
6. Run any missing migrations using Supabase CLI if needed
tasks.testStrategy: Create automated script to verify schema matches expected structure. Test RLS policies by attempting cross-firm data access. Verify foreign key constraints are enforced.

subtasks.title: Connect to Supabase and Inspect Schema
subtasks.description: Establish connection to live Supabase instance and run schema inspection queries
subtasks.details: Connect to the live Supabase instance using appropriate credentials. Execute the schema inspection SQL query to retrieve all table names in the public schema. Store the results for comparison against expected tables.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify connection succeeds and query returns results. Test with read-only credentials to ensure no accidental modifications.

subtasks.title: Compare Tables Against Expected Schema
subtasks.description: Compare discovered tables with the expected 28 tables from migrations
subtasks.details: Create a list of the 28 expected tables from the migration files. Compare this list against the tables found in step 1. Identify any missing tables or unexpected additional tables. Document discrepancies.
subtasks.testStrategy: Use automated comparison script to detect differences. Verify script correctly identifies both missing and extra tables.

subtasks.title: Verify Row Counts for All Tables
subtasks.description: Check row counts for each table to understand data population status
subtasks.details: For each table discovered, execute COUNT(*) queries to determine row counts. Pay special attention to critical tables like users, firms, documents, etc. Identify empty tables that may need seeding.
subtasks.testStrategy: Ensure count queries complete without timeout. Verify counts are reasonable (no negative or extremely large values).

subtasks.title: Audit RLS Policies and Security
subtasks.description: Verify Row Level Security policies exist and are properly configured
subtasks.details: Query pg_policies to list all RLS policies in the public schema. Verify each table has appropriate policies for SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE operations. Check that policies enforce proper firm-level data isolation.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test RLS by attempting cross-firm data access with different user contexts. Verify policies block unauthorized access.

subtasks.title: Document Findings and Run Missing Migrations
subtasks.description: Create comprehensive audit report and apply any missing database changes
subtasks.details: Create database_audit.md file documenting all findings including table list, row counts, missing tables, and RLS policy status. If any migrations are missing, use Supabase CLI to apply them. Include remediation steps for any issues found.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify markdown file is properly formatted and readable. Test migration rollback/forward if changes are needed.

tasks.title: Create AML Document Ingestion Core Module
tasks.description: Build the core Python module for downloading and processing AML PDF documents from aml.gov.sa with proper Arabic URL encoding and error handling
tasks.details:
Create `backend/ingest_aml.py` with the following implementation:

```python
import requests
import urllib.parse
from pathlib import Path
import hashlib
from datetime import datetime
from typing import List, Dict, Optional
import logging
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
from models import Document, Chunk
from ingest import extract_text_from_pdf, chunk_text, generate_embeddings

class AMLDocumentIngester:
    def __init__(self, db_session: Session):
        self.session = db_session
        self.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
        self.temp_dir = Path('temp/aml_downloads')
        self.temp_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        
    def download_pdf(self, url: str, filename: str) -> Optional[Path]:
        """Download PDF handling Arabic URL encoding"""
        try:
            # Properly encode Arabic characters in URL
            parsed = urllib.parse.urlparse(url)
            encoded_path = urllib.parse.quote(parsed.path.encode('utf-8'))
            encoded_url = f"{parsed.scheme}://{parsed.netloc}{encoded_path}"
            
            response = requests.get(encoded_url, timeout=60, verify=False)
            response.raise_for_status()
            
            file_path = self.temp_dir / filename
            file_path.write_bytes(response.content)
            return file_path
        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.error(f"Failed to download {url}: {e}")
            return None
            
    def check_duplicate(self, source_url: str) -> bool:
        """Check if document already ingested"""
        existing = self.session.query(Document).filter_by(source_url=source_url).first()
        return existing is not None
        
    def ingest_document(self, doc_info: Dict) -> Dict:
        """Ingest single document following existing pipeline"""
        if self.check_duplicate(doc_info['url']):
            return {'status': 'skipped', 'reason': 'already_exists'}
            
        # Download PDF
        pdf_path = self.download_pdf(doc_info['url'], doc_info['filename'])
        if not pdf_path:
            return {'status': 'failed', 'reason': 'download_failed'}
            
        try:
            # Extract text
            text_content = extract_text_from_pdf(str(pdf_path))
            
            # Create document record
            doc = Document(
                title=doc_info['title'],
                content=text_content[:1000],  # Store preview
                source='aml.gov.sa',
                source_url=doc_info['url'],
                doc_type=doc_info['category'],
                created_at=datetime.utcnow()
            )
            self.session.add(doc)
            self.session.flush()
            
            # Chunk and embed
            chunks = chunk_text(text_content, chunk_size=1000, overlap=200)
            chunk_records = []
            
            for idx, chunk_text in enumerate(chunks):
                embedding = generate_embeddings(chunk_text)
                chunk = Chunk(
                    document_id=doc.id,
                    content=chunk_text,
                    embedding=embedding,
                    chunk_index=idx
                )
                chunk_records.append(chunk)
                
            self.session.bulk_save_objects(chunk_records)
            self.session.commit()
            
            # Cleanup
            pdf_path.unlink()
            
            return {
                'status': 'success',
                'document_id': doc.id,
                'chunks_created': len(chunks)
            }
            
        except Exception as e:
            self.session.rollback()
            self.logger.error(f"Failed to ingest {doc_info['title']}: {e}")
            return {'status': 'failed', 'reason': str(e)}
```
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit test download_pdf with mock requests to verify Arabic URL encoding
2. Test check_duplicate with mock database queries
3. Integration test full ingestion pipeline with small test PDF
4. Verify chunk creation and embedding generation
5. Test error handling for network failures and invalid PDFs
6. Validate deduplication logic prevents re-ingestion

tasks.title: Create AML Ingestion API Endpoint
tasks.description: Implement authenticated API endpoint to trigger AML document ingestion with progress tracking and admin-only access control
tasks.details:
Add to `backend/api/dashboard.py`:

```python
from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, BackgroundTasks
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
from auth import get_current_user, require_admin
from database import get_db
from ingest_aml import AMLDocumentIngester
from aml_documents import get_aml_documents
import asyncio
from typing import Dict
import uuid

# In-memory progress tracking
ingestion_progress = {}

@router.post("/api/dashboard/ingest-aml")
async def ingest_aml_documents(
    background_tasks: BackgroundTasks,
    current_user: dict = Depends(require_admin),
    db: Session = Depends(get_db)
):
    """Trigger AML document ingestion (admin only)"""
    
    # Generate task ID for progress tracking
    task_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
    
    # Initialize progress
    ingestion_progress[task_id] = {
        'status': 'started',
        'total': 10,
        'completed': 0,
        'failed': 0,
        'skipped': 0,
        'details': []
    }
    
    # Run ingestion in background
    background_tasks.add_task(
        run_aml_ingestion,
        task_id=task_id,
        db=db
    )
    
    return {
        'task_id': task_id,
        'message': 'AML document ingestion started',
        'total_documents': 10
    }

async def run_aml_ingestion(task_id: str, db: Session):
    """Background task to ingest all AML documents"""
    ingester = AMLDocumentIngester(db)
    documents = get_aml_documents()
    
    for idx, doc in enumerate(documents):
        try:
            # Update progress
            ingestion_progress[task_id]['status'] = f'Processing {idx+1}/10'
            
            # Ingest document
            result = ingester.ingest_document(doc)
            
            # Update counters
            if result['status'] == 'success':
                ingestion_progress[task_id]['completed'] += 1
            elif result['status'] == 'skipped':
                ingestion_progress[task_id]['skipped'] += 1
            else:
                ingestion_progress[task_id]['failed'] += 1
                
            # Add to details
            ingestion_progress[task_id]['details'].append({
                'document': doc['title'],
                'result': result
            })
            
        except Exception as e:
            ingestion_progress[task_id]['failed'] += 1
            ingestion_progress[task_id]['details'].append({
                'document': doc['title'],
                'result': {'status': 'error', 'message': str(e)}
            })
            
    ingestion_progress[task_id]['status'] = 'completed'
    
@router.get("/api/dashboard/ingest-aml/{task_id}")
async def get_ingestion_progress(
    task_id: str,
    current_user: dict = Depends(get_current_user)
):
    """Get progress of AML ingestion task"""
    if task_id not in ingestion_progress:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Task not found")
        
    return ingestion_progress[task_id]
```
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test endpoint requires admin authentication
2. Verify background task starts and returns task_id
3. Test progress endpoint returns real-time updates
4. Mock ingestion process to test success/skip/fail scenarios
5. Test concurrent ingestion requests are handled
6. Verify memory cleanup for old task progress data

tasks.title: Implement High Risk Countries Web Scraper
tasks.description: Create a scraper for the AML high risk countries page that extracts country names and risk classifications into structured data
tasks.details:
Create `backend/scrapers/aml_countries_scraper.py`:

```python
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
from datetime import datetime
from typing import List, Dict
import logging
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
from models import Base, Column, Integer, String, DateTime, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

# Define new model for high risk countries
class HighRiskCountry(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'high_risk_countries'
    
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    country_name_ar = Column(String(255))
    country_name_en = Column(String(255))
    risk_category = Column(String(50))  # 'high_risk', 'under_monitoring'
    source = Column(String(50), default='aml.gov.sa')
    is_active = Column(Boolean, default=True)
    last_updated = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)
    
class AMLCountriesScraper:
    def __init__(self, db_session: Session):
        self.session = db_session
        self.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
        self.url = 'https://www.aml.gov.sa/ar-sa/Pages/HighRiskCountries.aspx'
        
    def scrape_countries(self) -> List[Dict]:
        """Scrape high risk countries from AML website"""
        try:
            response = requests.get(self.url, timeout=30, verify=False)
            response.raise_for_status()
            
            soup = BeautifulSoup(response.content, 'html.parser')
            countries = []
            
            # Find high risk countries section
            high_risk_section = soup.find('div', {'class': 'high-risk-countries'})
            if high_risk_section:
                country_items = high_risk_section.find_all('li')
                for item in country_items:
                    countries.append({
                        'name': item.text.strip(),
                        'category': 'high_risk'
                    })
                    
            # Find countries under monitoring section
            monitoring_section = soup.find('div', {'class': 'monitoring-countries'})
            if monitoring_section:
                country_items = monitoring_section.find_all('li')
                for item in country_items:
                    countries.append({
                        'name': item.text.strip(),
                        'category': 'under_monitoring'
                    })
                    
            return countries
            
        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.error(f"Failed to scrape countries: {e}")
            return []
            
    def update_database(self, countries: List[Dict]):
        """Update high risk countries in database"""
        try:
            # Mark all existing as inactive
            self.session.query(HighRiskCountry).update({'is_active': False})
            
            # Insert or update countries
            for country in countries:
                existing = self.session.query(HighRiskCountry).filter_by(
                    country_name_ar=country['name']
                ).first()
                
                if existing:
                    existing.is_active = True
                    existing.risk_category = country['category']
                    existing.last_updated = datetime.utcnow()
                else:
                    new_country = HighRiskCountry(
                        country_name_ar=country['name'],
                        risk_category=country['category'],
                        is_active=True
                    )
                    self.session.add(new_country)
                    
            self.session.commit()
            self.logger.info(f"Updated {len(countries)} high risk countries")
            
        except Exception as e:
            self.session.rollback()
            self.logger.error(f"Failed to update database: {e}")
            
    def get_active_countries(self) -> List[HighRiskCountry]:
        """Get all active high risk countries"""
        return self.session.query(HighRiskCountry).filter_by(is_active=True).all()
```
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Mock HTML response to test parsing logic
2. Test extraction of both high risk and monitoring categories
3. Verify database update marks old entries as inactive
4. Test handling of duplicate countries
5. Validate error handling for network failures
6. Test get_active_countries returns only current data

tasks.title: Extend Periodic Scraper for AML.gov.sa
tasks.description: Add AML.gov.sa to the existing periodic scraping infrastructure to check for new documents and updates alongside CMA scraping
tasks.details:
Create `backend/scrapers/aml_document_scraper.py`:

```python
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
from datetime import datetime
from typing import List, Dict, Optional
import hashlib
from urllib.parse import urljoin
import logging
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
from models import Alert, Document

class AMLDocumentScraper:
    def __init__(self, db_session: Session):
        self.session = db_session
        self.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
        self.base_url = 'https://www.aml.gov.sa'
        self.pages_to_check = [
            '/ar-sa/RulesAndRegulations/Pages/default.aspx',
            '/ar-sa/RulesAndInstructions/Pages/default.aspx',
            '/ar-sa/GuidanceReports/Pages/default.aspx'
        ]
        
    def scrape_for_updates(self) -> List[Dict]:
        """Check AML website for new or updated documents"""
        all_findings = []
        
        for page_path in self.pages_to_check:
            url = urljoin(self.base_url, page_path)
            findings = self._scrape_page(url)
            all_findings.extend(findings)
            
        return all_findings
        
    def _scrape_page(self, url: str) -> List[Dict]:
        """Scrape individual page for documents"""
        try:
            response = requests.get(url, timeout=30, verify=False)
            response.raise_for_status()
            
            soup = BeautifulSoup(response.content, 'html.parser')
            findings = []
            
            # Find all PDF links
            pdf_links = soup.find_all('a', href=lambda x: x and x.endswith('.pdf'))
            
            for link in pdf_links:
                pdf_url = urljoin(self.base_url, link.get('href'))
                title = link.text.strip() or 'Untitled Document'
                
                # Check if document exists in database
                existing = self.session.query(Document).filter_by(
                    source_url=pdf_url
                ).first()
                
                if not existing:
                    findings.append({
                        'type': 'new_document',
                        'url': pdf_url,
                        'title': title,
                        'found_at': datetime.utcnow()
                    })
                else:
                    # Check if document was updated (would need file metadata)
                    # For now, skip update detection
                    pass
                    
            return findings
            
        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.error(f"Failed to scrape {url}: {e}")
            return []
            
    def save_alerts(self, findings: List[Dict]):
        """Save new findings as alerts"""
        for finding in findings:
            alert = Alert(
                source='aml.gov.sa',
                alert_type=finding['type'],
                title=f"New AML Document: {finding['title']}",
                description=f"Found new document at {finding['url']}",
                url=finding['url'],
                created_at=finding['found_at']
            )
            self.session.add(alert)
            
        self.session.commit()
        self.logger.info(f"Saved {len(findings)} new AML alerts")

# Update the main scheduler to include AML scraping
def update_scheduler():
    """Add to existing scheduler.py"""
    from scrapers.aml_document_scraper import AMLDocumentScraper
    from scrapers.aml_countries_scraper import AMLCountriesScraper
    
    # In the daily job function:
    def daily_scraping_job():
        with get_db() as db:
            # Existing CMA scraping...
            
            # Add AML document scraping
            aml_doc_scraper = AMLDocumentScraper(db)
            aml_findings = aml_doc_scraper.scrape_for_updates()
            if aml_findings:
                aml_doc_scraper.save_alerts(aml_findings)
                
            # Add high risk countries update
            aml_countries_scraper = AMLCountriesScraper(db)
            countries = aml_countries_scraper.scrape_countries()
            if countries:
                aml_countries_scraper.update_database(countries)
```
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Mock AML website responses to test document discovery
2. Test deduplication against existing documents
3. Verify alert creation for new findings
4. Test integration with existing scheduler
5. Validate error handling doesn't break other scrapers
6. Test performance with multiple page scraping

tasks.title: Update Chat System Prompt for AML Knowledge
tasks.description: Modify the chat system prompt to include AML/CTF regulatory knowledge and ensure RAG retrieval covers both CMA and AML documents
tasks.details:
Update `backend/chat/system_prompts.py` and RAG configuration:

```python
# Update system prompt
COMPLIANCE_SYSTEM_PROMPT = """
You are TAM AI, an expert compliance assistant specializing in Saudi Arabian financial regulations.

Your knowledge base includes:
1. Capital Market Authority (CMA) regulations and circulars
2. Anti-Money Laundering (AML) and Counter-Terrorist Financing (CTF) regulations from SAFIU
3. SAMA (Saudi Central Bank) regulations
4. International compliance standards (FATF, Basel)

Key areas of expertise:
- Securities and investment regulations
- AML/CTF compliance requirements
- Risk-based approach methodologies
- Beneficial ownership requirements
- Suspicious transaction reporting
- Customer due diligence (CDD) and enhanced due diligence (EDD)
- Proliferation financing risks
- High-risk jurisdictions and sanctions

When answering questions:
1. Always cite specific articles, regulations, or guidance documents
2. Distinguish between CMA requirements and AML/SAFIU requirements
3. Provide practical implementation guidance
4. Consider both Arabic and English terminology
5. Reference the most recent versions of regulations

If asked about high-risk countries, refer to the official SAFIU list.
For AML/CTF matters, prioritize SAFIU regulations and guidance.
For capital market matters, prioritize CMA regulations.
"""

# Update RAG retrieval to search both sources
def update_rag_search():
    """Modify backend/chat/rag_service.py"""
    
    def search_knowledge_base(query: str, limit: int = 5) -> List[Dict]:
        """Enhanced search across all data sources"""
        
        # Generate query embedding
        query_embedding = generate_embeddings(query)
        
        # Search across all sources with source weighting
        results = db.query(
            Chunk.id,
            Chunk.content,
            Chunk.document_id,
            Document.title,
            Document.source,
            Document.doc_type,
            func.sqrt(
                func.sum(
                    func.pow(Chunk.embedding - query_embedding, 2)
                )
            ).label('distance')
        ).join(
            Document, Chunk.document_id == Document.id
        ).filter(
            Document.source.in_(['cma.gov.sa', 'aml.gov.sa'])
        ).order_by(
            'distance'
        ).limit(limit * 2).all()  # Get more results for filtering
        
        # Ensure diversity of sources in results
        cma_results = [r for r in results if r.source == 'cma.gov.sa']
        aml_results = [r for r in results if r.source == 'aml.gov.sa']
        
        # Balance results from both sources
        final_results = []
        for i in range(limit):
            if i < len(cma_results):
                final_results.append(cma_results[i])
            if i < len(aml_results) and len(final_results) < limit:
                final_results.append(aml_results[i])
                
        return final_results[:limit]

# Add source-specific context formatting
def format_context(chunks: List[Dict]) -> str:
    """Format retrieved chunks with source attribution"""
    context_parts = []
    
    for chunk in chunks:
        source_label = "CMA" if chunk['source'] == 'cma.gov.sa' else "AML/SAFIU"
        context_parts.append(
            f"[Source: {source_label} - {chunk['title']}]\n"
            f"{chunk['content']}\n\n"
        )
        
    return "\n".join(context_parts)
```

1. Test chat with AML-specific questions
2. Verify citations include both CMA and AML sources
3. Test source diversity in RAG results
4. Validate handling of Arabic terms in queries
5. Test high-risk country queries use SAFIU data
6. Benchmark retrieval performance with expanded corpus

tasks.title: Implement Database Migrations for New Tables
tasks.description: Create Alembic migrations for the high_risk_countries table and any schema updates needed for AML data integration
tasks.details:
Create migration file `backend/alembic/versions/xxx_add_aml_tables.py`:

```python
"""Add AML related tables and columns

Revision ID: xxx_add_aml_tables
Revises: previous_revision_id
Create Date: 2024-01-XX

"""
from alembic import op
import sqlalchemy as sa
from sqlalchemy.dialects import postgresql

# revision identifiers
revision = 'xxx_add_aml_tables'
down_revision = 'previous_revision_id'
branch_labels = None
depends_on = None

def upgrade():
    # Create high_risk_countries table
    op.create_table(
        'high_risk_countries',
        sa.Column('id', sa.Integer(), nullable=False),
        sa.Column('country_name_ar', sa.String(255), nullable=True),
        sa.Column('country_name_en', sa.String(255), nullable=True),
        sa.Column('risk_category', sa.String(50), nullable=True),
        sa.Column('source', sa.String(50), server_default='aml.gov.sa'),
        sa.Column('is_active', sa.Boolean(), server_default='true'),
        sa.Column('last_updated', sa.DateTime(), server_default=sa.func.now()),
        sa.PrimaryKeyConstraint('id')
    )
    
    # Add indexes for performance
    op.create_index(
        'idx_high_risk_countries_active',
        'high_risk_countries',
        ['is_active']
    )
    
    op.create_index(
        'idx_high_risk_countries_category',
        'high_risk_countries',
        ['risk_category']
    )
    
    # Add source index to documents table if not exists
    op.create_index(
        'idx_documents_source',
        'documents',
        ['source'],
        postgresql_using='btree'
    )
    
    # Add doc_type index for category filtering
    op.create_index(
        'idx_documents_doc_type',
        'documents',
        ['doc_type'],
        postgresql_using='btree'
    )
    
    # Add composite index for source queries
    op.create_index(
        'idx_documents_source_created',
        'documents',
        ['source', 'created_at'],
        postgresql_using='btree'
    )
    
    # Update existing documents to ensure source field is populated
    op.execute("""
        UPDATE documents 
        SET source = 'cma.gov.sa' 
        WHERE source IS NULL OR source = ''
    """)
    
def downgrade():
    # Drop indexes
    op.drop_index('idx_documents_source_created', 'documents')
    op.drop_index('idx_documents_doc_type', 'documents')
    op.drop_index('idx_documents_source', 'documents')
    op.drop_index('idx_high_risk_countries_category', 'high_risk_countries')
    op.drop_index('idx_high_risk_countries_active', 'high_risk_countries')
    
    # Drop table
    op.drop_table('high_risk_countries')

# Create migration command script
def create_migration_script():
    """backend/scripts/run_aml_migration.py"""
    import subprocess
    import sys
    
    def run_migration():
        try:
            # Generate migration
            subprocess.run([
                'alembic', 'revision', '--autogenerate',
                '-m', 'Add AML tables and indexes'
            ], check=True)
            
            # Run migration
            subprocess.run(['alembic', 'upgrade', 'head'], check=True)
            
            print("Migration completed successfully")
            
        except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
            print(f"Migration failed: {e}")
            sys.exit(1)
            
    if __name__ == '__main__':
        run_migration()
```
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test migration on development database copy
2. Verify rollback (downgrade) works correctly
3. Test indexes improve query performance
4. Validate data integrity after migration
5. Test migration with existing data present
6. Verify all foreign key constraints are maintained

tasks.title: Create Integration Tests and Documentation
tasks.description: Develop comprehensive integration tests for the AML ingestion pipeline and create documentation for maintenance and operations
tasks.details:
Create test suite and documentation: ```python # backend/tests/test_aml_integration.py import pytest from unittest.mock import Mock, patch, MagicMock import tempfile from pathlib import Path from sqlalchemy import create_engine from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker from models import Base, Document, Chunk, HighRiskCountry from ingest_aml import AMLDocumentIngester from aml_documents import get_aml_documents from scrapers.aml_countries_scraper import AMLCountriesScraper @pytest.fixture def test_db(): """Create test database""" engine = create_engine('sqlite:///:memory:') Base.metadata.create_all(engine) SessionLocal = sessionmaker(bind=engine) session = SessionLocal() yield session session.close() @pytest.fixture def mock_pdf_content(): """Create mock PDF for testing""" return b'%PDF-1.4\nMock AML regulation content\nArticle 1: Test content' class TestAMLIngestion: def test_document_download_with_arabic_url(self, test_db, mock_pdf_content): """Test downloading PDF with Arabic characters in URL""" ingester = AMLDocumentIngester(test_db) with patch('requests.get') as mock_get: mock_response = Mock() mock_response.content = mock_pdf_content mock_response.raise_for_status = Mock() mock_get.return_value = mock_response url = 'https://example.com/اللائحة التنفيذية.pdf' result = ingester.download_pdf(url, 'test.pdf') assert result is not None assert result.exists() def test_duplicate_detection(self, test_db): """Test that duplicate documents are not re-ingested""" # Insert existing document existing_doc = Document( title='Test Document', source='aml.gov.sa', source_url='https://test.com/doc.pdf' ) test_db.add(existing_doc) test_db.commit() ingester = AMLDocumentIngester(test_db) assert ingester.check_duplicate('https://test.com/doc.pdf') == True assert ingester.check_duplicate('https://test.com/new.pdf') == False def test_full_ingestion_pipeline(self, test_db, mock_pdf_content): """Test complete document ingestion process""" ingester = AMLDocumentIngester(test_db) with patch.object(ingester, 'download_pdf') as mock_download: mock_path = Path(tempfile.mktemp(suffix='.pdf')) mock_path.write_bytes(mock_pdf_content) mock_download.return_value = mock_path with patch('ingest.extract_text_from_pdf') as mock_extract: mock_extract.return_value = 'Test document content' with patch('ingest.generate_embeddings') as mock_embed: mock_embed.return_value = [0.1] * 768 doc_info = { 'title': 'Test AML Document', 'url': 'https://test.com/doc.pdf', 'category': 'regulation', 'filename': 'test.pdf' } result = ingester.ingest_document(doc_info) assert result['status'] == 'success' assert 'document_id' in result assert result['chunks_created'] > 0 # Verify database records doc = test_db.query(Document).first() assert doc is not None assert doc.title == 'Test AML Document' chunks = test_db.query(Chunk).all() assert len(chunks) > 0 class TestHighRiskCountriesScraper: def test_country_extraction(self, test_db): """Test extraction of countries from HTML""" scraper = AMLCountriesScraper(test_db) mock_html = """
  • إيران
  • كوريا الشمالية
  • باكستان
  • سوريا
""" with patch('requests.get') as mock_get: mock_response = Mock() mock_response.content = mock_html.encode('utf-8') mock_get.return_value = mock_response countries = scraper.scrape_countries() assert len(countries) == 4 assert any(c['name'] == 'إيران' and c['category'] == 'high_risk' for c in countries) assert any(c['name'] == 'باكستان' and c['category'] == 'under_monitoring' for c in countries) def test_database_update(self, test_db): """Test updating countries in database""" scraper = AMLCountriesScraper(test_db) countries = [ {'name': 'Country A', 'category': 'high_risk'}, {'name': 'Country B', 'category': 'under_monitoring'} ] scraper.update_database(countries) # Verify insertion db_countries = test_db.query(HighRiskCountry).all() assert len(db_countries) == 2 assert all(c.is_active for c in db_countries) # Create documentation def create_documentation(): """backend/docs/aml_integration.md""" documentation = """ # AML.gov.sa Integration Documentation ## Overview This module integrates Saudi AML Authority (SAFIU) documents and data into the TAM Compliance AI system. ## Components ### 1. Document Ingestion (`ingest_aml.py`) - Downloads PDFs from aml.gov.sa - Handles Arabic URL encoding - Processes documents through existing RAG pipeline - Prevents duplicate ingestion ### 2. High Risk Countries Scraper - Scrapes official high-risk jurisdictions list - Updates database with current country classifications - Runs as part of daily scheduled jobs ### 3. API Endpoints - `POST /api/dashboard/ingest-aml`: Trigger manual ingestion (admin only) - `GET /api/dashboard/ingest-aml/{task_id}`: Check ingestion progress - `GET /api/dashboard/data-sources`: Get statistics for all sources ## Configuration ### Environment Variables ```bash # No additional environment variables required # Uses existing database and embedding configurations ``` ### Document Registry All AML documents are defined in `aml_documents.py`: - 10 regulatory PDFs - Categories: regulation, guidance, report - Bilingual titles (Arabic/English) ## Operations ### Manual Ingestion 1. Admin users can trigger ingestion from dashboard 2. Progress is tracked in real-time 3. Failed documents are logged but don't stop the process ### Scheduled Updates - Daily scraping checks for new documents - High-risk countries list updated daily - Alerts created for new findings ## Troubleshooting ### Common Issues 1. **SSL Certificate Errors**: Set verify=False for aml.gov.sa requests 2. **Arabic URL Encoding**: URLs are properly encoded before requests 3. **Large PDFs**: Timeout set to 60 seconds for downloads ### Monitoring - Check dashboard for ingestion status - Review logs for failed documents - Monitor chunk creation in database ## Testing Run integration tests: ```bash pytest tests/test_aml_integration.py -v ``` ## Maintenance - Periodically verify all document URLs are still valid - Update document registry if new regulations published - Monitor disk space for temporary PDF downloads """ with open('backend/docs/aml_integration.md', 'w') as f: f.write(documentation) ```
